title: TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS - 211info
text: OREGON TRADESWOMEN TRADES AND APPRENTICESHIP CAREER CLASS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a 7-week, state-certified, pre-apprenticeship training class in the skilled construction trades that provides students with the following: -Learn basic trades, math, and measurement -Learn to use hand and power tools -Receive an introduction to green building -Explore topics such as job site safety and construction culture -Gain 30 hours of hands-on experience working alongside skilled female instructors on actual job sites -Field trips to apprenticeship training centers and active construction sites -Improve physical fitness with strength training taught by a certified fitness trainer Career counselors assist program graduates with job searches and application processes to apprenticeship training programs upon graduation. Career counselors also provide individualized employment counseling throughout the entire course of the graduate's trades career. 16790 NE Mason Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: -Women and other gender minorities -Age 18 years or older -GED or high school diploma -Ability to pass a drug test -Attend an information session -Take the National Career Readiness Certificate (NCRC) test Additional enrollment requirements may be required and are available during the initial informational session Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (503) 335-8200 121 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 335-8200 ext 121 FAX (503) 249-0445 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or call for more information, schedule an information session, current schedule, and session opening dates FEES: None DOCUMENTS: A resume and/or other detailed information regarding job history is helpful to bring to the information session but is not required AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 87 stops at NE Airport Way and Mason Court, bus 74 stops NE 166th and NE Airport Way Services: Job Training Formats * Women Occupation Specific Job Training * Women Prejob Guidance * Women If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON TRADESWOMEN
